title: senecio × peregrinus
intro: Senecio × peregrinus, also known as Buttercup Ragwort or Common Ragwort, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Senecio jacobaea and Senecio aureus, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, particularly jacolin and peregrin. The health benefits of Senecio × peregrinus include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a topical application to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Senecio × peregrinus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although its consumption should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
